title: Endpoint, Mobile, Browser, Wireless, and SOHO Hardening · Evidence Workshop
summary: A guided support bench extends layered endpoint hardening and exception register while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered endpoint hardening and exception register while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.
Choose supported personal or enterprise wireless authentication, encryption, credentials, certificates, onboarding, rotation, revocation, and guest isolation.
Apply screen lock, supported updates, encryption, secure enrollment, app sources, permissions, backups, remote response, connectivity controls, and privacy-aware management.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hardening Baseline
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hardening Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hardening Baseline, keep Hardening Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
